urgency.message: 4 critical CVE(s) and 5 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: CISA directive for AI executive order to be released this week, Andersen says
items.summary: The binding operational directive will focus in part on “vulnerability alleviation and vulnerability management,” Andersen said in remarks delivered at the TechNet Cyber conference in Baltimore.

items.title: Software supply chain attacks: check your dependencies
items.summary: Attackers are compromising open-source packages to spread malware. Cyber defenders are asked to review dependencies to reduce risks

items.summary: Cybersecurity and business leaders with experience of dealing with major incidents from within the NCSC and at JLR detail what you need to prioritize if your organization is hit by a cyber-attack

items.summary: Former Ukrainian foreign minister, Dmytro Kuleba, urges Infosecurity Europe attendees to fight the good fight

items.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical flaw impacting Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento full-page cache extension, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation in the wild.

items.summary:
The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Wednesday announced the results of a sweeping action undertaken by government authorities and private sector companies to combat cyber-enabled and cryptocurrency fraud targeting Americans.

items.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
items.summary: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity.

Chinese hackers use new Atlas RAT malware in European cyberattacks
items.summary: A Chinese-speaking cybercrime group has expanded its targeting to the European space, deploying previously undocumented malware and the Atlas backdoor. [...]

items.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
items.summary: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.

items.title: U.S. sanctions Nobitex crypto exchange used by Iranian ransomware actors
items.summary: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has announced sanctions against Nobitex, Iran's largest cryptocurrency exchange, for facilitating payments related to terrorist activities. [...]

items.title: CISA warns of cyberattacks targeting fuel tank monitoring systems
items.summary: CISA, the FBI, the NSA, the Department of Energy, and other US government partners are warning that hackers are targeting internet-exposed automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems used to monitor fuel and liquid storage tanks across various critical infrastructure sectors. [...]

items.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
items.summary: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.

items.title: WhatsApp, Slack Notifications Could Hijack Google Gemini on Android
items.summary: A single poisoned notification from WhatsApp, Slack, SMS, Signal, Instagram, or Messenger could have hijacked Google Gemini's voice assistant on Android and made it open a victim's connected windows, fake a message from their boss, push the phone into a Zoom call, or quietly poison its long-term mem

items.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
items.summary: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.

items.title: New 'HTTP/2 Bomb' DoS attack crashes web servers in under a minute
items.summary: A new denial-of-service (DoS) attack dubbed HTTP/2 Bomb can be launched from a single machine to take down web servers within seconds. [...]

items.title: DHS chief signals efforts to reshape CISA
items.summary: In his first appearance before the panel since being confirmed in March, Mullin said that CISA probably needs “somewhere around” 2,800 employees, despite its ability to hire up to 3,400.

items.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
items.summary: A disabled security setting meant to protect authentication across Android versions of key apps like Word, PowerPoint, and Excel paved the way for attackers to steal logins and data.

items.title: xAI Asks Court to Strip Alleged Grok Deepfake Nudes Victims of Anonymity
items.summary: Four people suing Elon Musk's AI firm under pseudonyms due to the risks of being identified may face a difficult choice: Reveal your real names, or drop the lawsuit.

items.title: Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform
items.summary:
Coralogix offers a full-stack observability platform that unifies logs, metrics, traces, security, and AI observability.
The post Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google DoubleClick Abused in New Malspam Campaign to Deliver DesckVB RAT
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malspam campaign that makes use of Google's DoubleClick domain as a way to evade detection and ultimately deliver a remote access trojan (RAT) named DesckVB RAT.

items.title: CISA warns of active attacks exploiting Android, Linux bugs
items.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in the Linux kernel and Android operating system. [...]

items.summary: Executive Summary Knowing what’s exploitable is only half the battle. items.title: Beyond the Zero-Day: See Your Network Like an Attacker | Webinar with HD Moore
items.summary:
Assume the breach. Zero-days keep shipping, AI is writing exploits faster than anyone patches, and "patch everything in time" stopped working years ago. Stop betting the org on winning that race. You don't control which bug lands. You control what it can reach once it does.

items.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
items.summary:
A development flag left switched on in production builds of several Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled the check that limits account-token sharing to trusted Microsoft apps.

Any other app on the same phone could ask for the signed-in user's token and get it, then read email, open files, browse the

items.title: What 345 Days of Untested Exposure Looks Like at a Bank
items.summary: A two-week penetration test can leave roughly 345 days of real-world exposure unvalidated. Sprocket Security explores why continuous testing is becoming critical as attack surfaces constantly change. [...]

items.title: Autonomous AI Tool Finds 2-Year-Old RCE Flaw in Redis (CVE-2026-23479)
items.summary:
Redis has  patched  a use-after-free in its blocking-client code that lets an authenticated user run arbitrary OS commands on the machine hosting the database. The flaw was found by an autonomous AI tool built to hunt bugs in large codebases.
Tracked as CVE-2026-23479, the flaw was introduced in Red

items.title: Continuing Scans for swagger.json, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
items.summary: Enterprise applications often still use complex standards like SOAP for web services. The big advantage of SOAP is its tight and extensive standards, which enable interoperability across an enterprise governed by web services. items.title: Kirki, Burst Statistics WordPress Plugin Flaws in Attackers’ Crosshairs
items.summary:
Threat actors are exploiting vulnerable Kirki and Burst Statistics deployments to elevate privileges and take over websites.

items.title: Security of 100 AI Agents Tested and Ranked – What You Need to Know
items.summary:
The AI Risk Quadrant evaluates AI agents based on three factors: how vulnerable they are to compromise, the potential impact of a breach, and the strength of their security defenses.
The post Security of 100 AI Agents Tested and Ranked – What You Need to Know appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: One-Click GitHub Dev Attack Lets Attackers Steal Full GitHub OAuth Tokens
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) that makes it possible to steal a user's GitHub token.

"Just by clicking a link, it's possible for an attacker to steal a GitHub token that can read and write to your repos, including private ones

items.title: Hackers Target Global Stock Exchange in Espionage Operation
items.summary:
The attackers had access to a senior executive’s email account for 150 days and exfiltrated data for months.
The post Hackers Target Global Stock Exchange in Espionage Operation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: IMA Diligence Services Data Breach Impacts 525,000 People
items.summary:
The affected individuals’ personal information was stolen from a legacy server managed by a third party.

items.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
items.summary: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.

An improper authentication bug allows attackers to escalate their privileges and escape containers.
